title: Topic, Headers, and Alternate Routing · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for topic, headers, and alternate routing and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a deterministic Go routing and unroutable-decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 6789 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Run the smallest deterministic RabbitMQ and Go messaging model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the quiz Topic Segment Design case
Inject one changed RabbitMQ and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-broker transfer limits for the quiz Topic Segment Design case

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Exchange Chain Cycle: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 6980 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle.
steps.why: Retrieving Exchange Chain Cycle through the concrete a deterministic Go routing and unroutable-decision table case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle, keep Exchange Chain Cycle connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Exchange Chain Cycle precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 6980 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use exchange-to-exchange bindings with bounded graph analysis, route evidence, permission checks, and cycle-safe topology review; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Exchange Chain Cycle, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Exchange chains cannot create duplicate or cyclic routing behavior. In Go, a nil publish error does not establish that any primary queue received the message.
